{"step_by_step_thinking": "Broad-spectrum antibiotics can alter the gut microbiota, leading to a reduction in vitamin K production. Since coumarin anticoagulants rely on vitamin K for their effectiveness, a reduction in vitamin K sources would lead to a reduced anticoagulant action. The documents mention that antimicrobial agents like metronidazole, macrolides, and fluoroquinolones can alter the intestinal microflora and decrease vitamin K absorption and synthesis. This supports the idea that broad-spectrum antibiotics can reduce the anticoagulant action by reducing vitamin K sources.", "answer_choice": "C"}
